content: What is the current status of getting the DRM abstractions merged? It seems like the mailing list sort of unfortunately dried up on a tense note https://lore.kernel.org/rust-for-linux/20230307-rust-drm-v1-0-917ff5bc80a8@asahilina.net/T/#t so I'm kind of hoping there is some progress somewhere that's not on the list

To understand the current state of the rust vgem and drm subsystem rust abstraction, I cherry-picked the relevant commits to the latest rust-next branch (Linux 6.6 RC1), and here are the results.

Some commits have been modified so that they can be built on a per-commit basis.
I cherry-picked the order of the commits as much as possible to match the order of the dependent commits so that the build doesn't break.

I shamelessly adopted vgem rust (Maíra Canal's implementation, Linux 6.2.0-rc4 base) commits and Ashahi Linux's DRM driver implementation (Linux 6.4.0 base)'s necessary and related rust layer commits as Monkey do.

  • Ashahi Linux
    - repo: https://github.com/AsahiLinux/linux.git
    - branch: gpu/rust-wip

  • VGEM Rust
    - repo: git@github.com:mairacanal/linux.git
    - branch: vgem/wip-dma

  • Worked repo/branch
    - https://github.com/elongbug/linux/tree/rust-next-drm-wip-vgem

When I run VGEM-related tests using the latest IGT, the results of the dmabuf_sync_file, vgem_basic, and vgem_slow tests are the same as Maíra Canal's VGEM test results. (All passes except kernel crash on vgem_basic-unload. ref: https://mairacanal.github.io/rust-for-vgem/ )

And I have a few questions as I go along, so I leave open questions.
@Asahi Lina Ask if you are working on platform drivers in platform.rs to build without OF or if someone is working on supporting pci drivers (to use APIs like pci_register_driver in drm drivers).

@Maíra Canal Are you under working on a fix to make driver unload of vgem rust driver work properly?

And is anyone working on a drm subsystem abstraction of ttm, drm_gpuva_manager, etc. for use in a rust gpu driver?

Lina did reply about this here https://rust-for-linux.zulipchat.com/#narrow/stream/288089-General/topic/Rust.20M1.20DRM.20driver.20runs.20GNOME!/near/390581894 (doesn't help you of course, just for context :smile:)
@Trevor Gross I hadn't read the thread before asking the question. Thanks for pointing it out. I'll take a closer look at that link as well.

Through zulip's search, I found work in progress on pci abstraction in the nvme implementation (https://github.com/metaspace/linux/tree/nvme).
And here I saw @Andreas Hindborg 's mention of common branches for rust-pci and rust-dma, which I think I should probably pay attention to avoid duplication of effort.

(Unfortunately, ttm, gpuva, etc. don't come up in the zulip search, so I guess it's a topic that hasn't been discussed in zulip).

Gwan-gyeong Mun said:

Through zulip's search, I found work in progress on pci abstraction in the nvme implementation (https://github.com/metaspace/linux/tree/nvme).
And here I saw Andreas Hindborg 's mention of common branches for rust-pci and rust-dma, which I think I should probably pay attention to avoid duplication of effort.

(Unfortunately, ttm, gpuva, etc. don't come up in the zulip search, so I guess it's a topic that hasn't been discussed in zulip).

Talking to people about setting up these branches is on my todo list, but I did not progress on it yet, sorry.
If we set them up, we should have links to these branches on rust-for-linux.com

Thanks for the comments.
I'll have to analyze the PCI-related abstraction of NVME's implementation before the common branch goes live.

Hi! I do plan to do gpuva soon, since I'll need that for my driver, but I haven't started on that yet. Once that and the replacement scheduler implementation are done I'll try to send things upstream again.

@Asahi Lina Thanks for sharing your plans.
I will keep paying attention to your next upstream patch series.

When I cherry-picked your patches, there was no implementation for the dynamic lock class key in upstream, so I changed the part that handles the dynamic lock class key to static and used it. What are your plans for the upstream implementation of the dynamic lock class key?

And I'm also wondering if your work includes making that platform drivers build even if they don't have OF dependencies.

I think the dynamic lockclass stuff is working pretty well, though it may or may not warrant more testing downstream? It didn't find any bugs in my driver yet but I think it will once we start caring about fence signaling rules (right now a lot of that is known-breaking-the-rules).

I haven't really looked into platform drivers or the driver core stuff. I just implemented most of the OF abstraction, but I think you'll want to talk to other people about the general driver/device model stuff.

Asahi Lina 말함:

I think the dynamic lockclass stuff is working pretty well, though it may or may not warrant more testing downstream? It didn't find any bugs in my driver yet but I think it will once we start caring about fence signaling rules (right now a lot of that is known-breaking-the-rules).

Thank you for sharing your thoughts.

Okay, I just checked your message on the XDC 2023 room. So, on this branch (https://github.com/mairacanal/linux/pull/11), the driver is 100% functional (as far as I know). But I need to update it to a new kernel and to the pin-init API.

Maíra Canal 말함:

Okay, I just checked your message on the XDC 2023 room. So, on this branch (https://github.com/mairacanal/linux/pull/11), the driver is 100% functional (as far as I know). But I need to update it to a new kernel and to the pin-init API.

@Maíra Canal Yes, I don't know if it meets your intent, but I migrated myself to use pin_init() to apply the above patches to the latest kernel.

I also took the same branch and applied it to the latest, did the driver unload work at that time?

@Maíra Canal In your implementation, if there was no problem with driver unload, I think I missed something when I brought your patches up to date. If not, I'd like to debug it so I can fix it. What do you think?

Gwan-gyeong Mun said:

Maíra Canal In your implementation, if there was no problem with driver unload, I think I missed something when I brought your patches up to date. If not, I'd like to debug it so I can fix it. What do you think?

From what I can remember, the driver unload used to work properly.
